Contact Rare & Manuscript Collections

Cornell athletics records, 1874-2016.
Collection Number: 40-1-370

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Cornell athletics records, 1874-2016.
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
40-1-370
Abstract:
Athletic event programs, photographs, annual reports, advertisements, plaques, cups, films and clippings concerning various Cornell athletic events, including women's athletics, and a certificate of incorporation of the Cornell Athletic Association.
Creator:
Cornell University
Quanitities:
50.6 cubic feet.
Language:
Collection material in English
Restrictions on Access

Folders for individual athletes and coaches may contain personally identifiable information or other sensative items and need to be reviewed before being made available.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Cornell Athletics Records, #40-1-370. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
RELATED MATERIALS

Related collections: Football Miscellany, 37-5-3981; Crew Records, 40-1-924; Hockey Films and Ephemera Collection, 40-4-3590; Football motion pictures, 40-2-2205; Track And Field Collection, 40-9-1820.
Scope and content

Athletic event programs and booklets, photographs, annual reports, advertisements, plaques, cups, films and clippings concerning various Cornell athletic events including women's athletics, and a certificate of incorporation of the Cornell Athletic Association. Also Cornell Victorious, the Cornell Campaign for Athletics, ca. 1990, V-790; Design plans for the Bartels Hall (formerly Alberding Field House), V-6028; Illini Reflections, ca. 1990 overview of past University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign events and life as presented by past university alumni. Included are Judi Ford-Johnson '73, Roger Ebert '64, Roger Plummer '84, Lester Leutwiler '29, Tom Haller '58, Ray Nitschke '58, and Dick Butkus '64, V-6029. Films include: Here We Are Again, 2 copies, ca. 1985, film about the history of Cornell athletics, F-478, 479; Stanford Today, 1985, film about Stanford University and its athletic programs, F-480. Also a film of a Cornell Soccer game ca. 1958-1960, F-390. Also slides from the 1974 Cornell and Cornellians presentation by Laurence Koplik.
Restrictions on Use:

Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage. Audio visual media that has been digitized: F-390.
Processing Information

April 2022: All material relating to Ice Hockey was transferred to the Cornell University Ice Hockey collection, #40-4-3590. This includes box 44, as well as various folders in other boxes. See Finding Aid for more information.

Names:

Cornell University -- : Sports.

Cornell University -- : Students.
Cornell University -- : Periodicals.
Subjects:
Athletics -- Study and teaching.
Women athletes.
Women college students.
Form and Genre Terms:
Programs (documents)
Photographs.

Series VII. Movie Films, Videos and other media
Box 9 Folder 1
Slides for Cornellians and Cornell presentation
 1974
Scope and Contents
Slide show made as part of a presentation by Laurence Koplik '72 and the Cornell Office of Public Information, visual Services and Radio-TV Film. Dedicated to Morris Bishop. Features many photos of various student activities on campus, but particularly athletic teams and coaches.
Box 9
Two photo albums showing renovation of Schoellkopf field, crescent bleachers, press box, Hoy field and Alumni fields.
 1988
Box 9
Binder of slides showing Athletic Facilities Study ca. 1983, shows maps of various Cornell athletics facilities and proposals for future expansion.
 1983
Box 9 V-790
Cornell Victorious, the Cornell Campaign for Athletics [VHS]
 1990
Box 9 V-6028
Design plans for the Bartels Hall (formerly Alberding Field House) [VHS]
 1988
Box 9 V-6029
Illini Reflections [VHS]
 1990
Scope and Contents
Overview of past University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign events and life as presented by past university alumni. Included are Judi Ford-Johnson '73, Roger Ebert '64, Roger Plummer '84, Lester Leutwiler '29, Tom Haller '58, Ray Nitschke '58, and Dick Butkus '64.
Box 9 F-390
Cornell Soccer game [16mm]
 1958-1960
Scope and Contents
Home soccer game on alumni field.
Box 9 F-478, F-479
Here We Are Again, 2 copies [16mm]
 1985
Scope and Contents
Film about the history of Cornell athletics.
Box 9 F-480
Stanford Today
 1985
16mm
Scope and Contents
Film about Stanford University and its athletic programs.
Box 5 Folder 3
Photos and negatives of inter-college athletic championship banners and plaques
 1910-1916
